title: Fresh Dense Uruguayan Beef Empanadas
description: A traditional Uruguayan dish featuring wonton skin wrapped around a dense beef filling, perfect for a savory snack or light meal. This one-pot recipe is easy to make and packed with flavor.
instructions: In a large pan over medium-high heat, cook the ground beef until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pan and cook until the onion is translucent.
Let the beef mixture cool to room temperature.
Lay a wonton skin on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of the skin.
Brush the edges of the skin with the beaten egg and fold the skin into a triangle, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada.
Repeat with the remaining wonton skins and beef mixture.
Heat about 1-2 inches of olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
When the oil is hot, add a few of the empanadas to the skillet (do not overcrowd) and cook until they are golden brown on the bottom.
Flip the empanadas and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until they are golden brown on the other side.
Remove the empanadas from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the empanadas warm or at room temperature.
